mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable LLM serving, leveraging advanced techniques like PagedAttention and continuous batching. Key trade-offs include the need for specific hardware configurations to fully utilize its capabilities.

steps.title: Add the vLLM Helm repository
steps.description:
This step adds the official vLLM Helm chart repository to your Helm configuration.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.16.0

```

> This adds the vLLM repository for version 0.16.0.

steps.title: Install vLLM CRDs
steps.description:
This step installs the Custom Resource Definitions required for vLLM to function properly.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.16.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This installs the necessary CRDs for vLLM.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This step installs the vLLM application in the specified namespace with the desired configuration.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.16.0

```

> This installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create an example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example Custom Resource (CR) to deploy a model using vLLM.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
EOF

```

> This creates a sample vLLM resource for a GPT-3 model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
EOF

steps.title: Verify the vLLM deployment
steps.description:
This step checks the status of the vLLM deployment and ensures that the Custom Resource is ready.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

kubectl get pods -n vllm

```

> This command lists the vLLM resources in the 'vllm' namespace.
> This command checks the status of the vLLM pods.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM deployment and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data associated with the vLLM resources.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you have a backup of your existing vLLM resources. This step will drain and cordon the nodes if necessary, perform the upgrade, and verify the deployment.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.16.0

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> Backup existing vLLM resources.
> This command upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> Verify that the vLLM resources are updated successfully.
commands: kubectl get vllm -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM Custom Resource Definition may not be installed.

**Fix:**
Run 'kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.16.0/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml' to install the CRDs.

troubleshooting.title: Pod is in 'CrashLoopBackOff' state
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM pod may be misconfigured or lacking resources.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n vllm' and adjust resource requests/limits as necessary.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough CPU resources available for vLLM.

**Fix:**
Ensure your cluster has sufficient resources or scale up your nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image may not exist or be accessible.

**Fix:**
Verify the image name and tag in your vLLM configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new model types and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see the vLLM resources listed with 'kubectl get vllm -n vllm' and the pods in 'Running' state with 'kubectl get pods -n vllm'.
codeSnippets:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster with the required resources and the necessary tools installed.
